description: Test cases for OBFUSCATION regex patterns (both HIGH_RISK and LOW_RISK). True positives should match; false positives should NOT match.

true_positives.reason: ROT13 decode of hidden command string

false_positives.text: # ROT13 is a simple cipher used for fun puzzles
false_positives.reason: Comment about ROT13, not actual usage

false_positives.text: # marshal.loads is dangerous, never use it

true_positives.reason: Building string character by character to hide 'eval'

false_positives.reason: Single chr() call for newline, not obfuscation
Arithmetic chr() in a loop, not string building

test_cases._note: LOW_RISK pattern — scanner skip rules exclude these from high-risk detection. Kept as documentation.

false_positives.reason: Hex-encoded eval — LOW_RISK, not flagged by scanner (skip rule)
Hex-encoded /bin/sh — LOW_RISK, not flagged by scanner (skip rule)
PNG magic bytes, only 2 hex escapes

test_cases._note: LOW_RISK pattern — scanner skip rules exclude these from high-risk detection. Kept as documentation.

true_positives.reason: Unicode-encoded 'system' — LOW_RISK but still detected by regex (6 consecutive escapes)

false_positives.reason: Unicode-encoded eval — scanner skip rules may exclude short sequences
Single unicode escape for snowman emoji
Single unicode escape for bullet point

false_positives.text: # base64.b64decode is used for auth tokens

true_positives.reason: Long base64 literal that could hide malicious content

false_positives.reason: Short string, not 60+ chars of base64

true_positives.reason: Building eval string from char codes

false_positives.text: // String.fromCharCode can be used for obfuscation
